source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 动荡的; 混乱的 full of confusion, violence, or disorder; not stable or steady: a turbulent crowd 3&3) # A## | a turbulent period of history 动荡的历史时期 i turbulence /ts:bjulons/ n. 3%; 混乱 a state of confusion, violence, or disorder: border turbulence 边境骚乱

headline.core_en: marked by disorder, instability, or violent movement

familiar_bridge.summary: turbulent 可以联想到 trouble 和 turmoil：不是普通的 busy，而是局面不稳、力量在翻动。

core.main: turbulent 描述政治、社会、情绪或水流空气处在不稳定、难控制的状态。

senses.label: 局势动荡
senses.text: 形容时期、社会、政治环境混乱不稳。

senses.label: 水流或气流湍急
senses.text: 形容水、空气或飞行过程强烈翻动。

usage_groups.group_title: 社会或政治局势动荡不稳
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调一个时期或环境充满冲突、变化和不确定性。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 政治、经济、历史、社会变化。

examples.sentence: The country entered a turbulent period after the sudden change in leadership.
examples.translation: 领导层突然更替后，这个国家进入了一段动荡时期。
examples.teaching_note: turbulent period 强调社会或政治环境不稳定。

usage_groups.group_title: 水流、空气或情绪剧烈翻动
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于具体的水流气流，也可延伸到人的情绪。

examples.sentence: The plane shook as it passed through turbulent air above the mountains.
examples.translation: 飞机穿过山上方的乱流时发生了颠簸。
examples.teaching_note: turbulent air 是航空语境中的自然表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The unstable market made investors cautious.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 不稳定的市场让投资者变得谨慎。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unstable 更普通，强调缺少稳定性。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The meeting became chaotic after the announcement.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 公告发布后，会议变得混乱。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The reform followed a tumultuous decade of protest.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这次改革发生在十年抗议动荡之后。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: tumultuous 常用于历史、政治或公共事件。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The region remained volatile despite the cease-fire.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 尽管停火，该地区仍然局势不稳。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: volatile 强调随时可能突然变化或爆发。

antonyms.example_sentence: The country enjoyed a stable political climate for decades.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个国家几十年来政治环境稳定。
antonyms.difference_note: stable 与 turbulent 的动荡相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The sea was calm after the storm.
antonyms.example_translation: 暴风雨过后海面恢复平静。
antonyms.difference_note: calm 可用于水面、情绪或局势。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The new rule created troublesome delays.
confusable_words.example_translation: 新规定造成了麻烦的延误。
confusable_words.difference_note: troublesome 指带来麻烦，不一定有动荡翻涌的状态。

extension.root_affix: turbulent 和 turmoil 都有混乱翻动的感觉；记忆时抓住“平稳状态被搅动”。

word_family.note: 名词，可指政治动荡或飞机遇到的乱流。
同属混浊不清的语义场，常指液体。

related_words.note: 常形容社会、政治或内心混乱。
per + turb，强调把平静状态打乱。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 4248; 怨恨 bitter indignation at having been treated unfairly: bear resentment against 对 … 怀恨 | have a feeling of resentment against sb. 对某人怀恨在心

headline.core_en: a bitter feeling caused by a sense of unfair treatment

familiar_bridge.summary: resentment 比 anger 更沉、更久，常来自“我被不公平对待”的感觉。

core.main: resentment 是压在心里的怨气，重点不是一时发火，而是因不公、轻视或伤害积累出的愤懑。

senses.label: 怨恨；愤懑
senses.text: 因觉得受到不公平对待而产生的持久不满。

usage_groups.group_title: 因不公待遇积累怨气
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 描述某人对人、制度或安排的不满长期存在。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 个人关系、社会政策、职场或群体冲突。

examples.sentence: Years of unequal pay created deep resentment among the workers.
examples.translation: 多年不平等的薪酬在工人中积累了深深的怨气。
examples.teaching_note: deep resentment 强调怨气长期积累。

examples.sentence: The policy generated resentment because many residents felt ignored.
examples.translation: 这项政策引发了怨愤，因为许多居民觉得自己被忽视。
examples.teaching_note: generate resentment 常用于社会或政治语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her anger was obvious during the debate.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 她在辩论中的愤怒很明显。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: anger 可以是一时情绪，resentment 更持久。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His bitterness grew after repeated rejection.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 反复被拒后，他的怨愤越来越深。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: bitterness 和 resentment 都有长期不满。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The decision provoked public indignation.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个决定激起了公众愤慨。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: indignation 更强调认为某事不公或不道德。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The dispute left years of rancor between the two families.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这场争执让两个家庭之间留下多年积怨。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: rancor 语气更重，敌意更深。

antonyms.example_sentence: The apology helped restore goodwill between the groups.
antonyms.example_translation: 道歉帮助恢复了两个群体之间的善意。
antonyms.difference_note: goodwill 与 resentment 的怨意相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: She felt gratitude rather than resentment toward her mentor.
antonyms.example_translation: 她对导师感到感激，而不是怨恨。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Resentment may lead to revenge, but the two are not the same.
confusable_words.example_translation: 怨恨可能导致报复，但两者不是一回事。
confusable_words.difference_note: resentment 是情绪，revenge 是报复行动。

extension.root_affix: resent 与 sense/sent 的“感觉”有联系；resentment 可以理解为对不公待遇反复感到不满。

word_family.note: 动词，常接 unfair treatment 或 someone’s interference。

related_words.note: 常因认为某事不公而愤怒。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt. SUE ( 传统 、 政权等 undermine the power and authority of (an established system or institution): They intended to subvert the lawful government of the country. 他们想要颠覆这个国家的合法政府 。 | subvert a dictatorship 推翻独裁政权

headline.core_en: to undermine or overturn an established system, authority, or belief

familiar_bridge.summary: vert 有“转”的感觉；subvert 像是从下面把一个制度、权威或观念的支撑转掉。

core.main: subvert 不是普通 damage，而是破坏某个已经建立的秩序、权威、规则或期待。

senses.label: 颠覆制度或权威
senses.text: 削弱或推翻已有体系。

senses.label: 颠覆期待或传统理解
senses.text: 文学艺术中常指有意打破惯常看法。

usage_groups.group_title: 破坏既有制度、权威或规则
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调从内部或根基上削弱一个系统。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 政治、法律、制度、组织权威。

examples.sentence: The group was accused of trying to subvert the government.
examples.translation: 这个组织被指试图颠覆政府。
examples.teaching_note: subvert the government 是政治语境中的强表达。

usage_groups.group_title: 打破读者或观众的预期
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 在评论文学、艺术或理论时，表示有意反转传统套路。

examples.sentence: The novel subverts the familiar hero story by making the supposed villain morally complex.
examples.translation: 这部小说把所谓反派写得道德上很复杂，从而颠覆了常见英雄故事。
examples.teaching_note: subvert expectations 常用于文学和影视评论。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The scandal undermined public trust.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 丑闻削弱了公众信任。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: undermine 常指逐渐削弱基础。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The court overturned the earlier decision.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 法院推翻了早先的判决。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: overturn 更强调结果上的推翻。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The crisis destabilized the fragile coalition.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 危机使脆弱的联盟不稳定。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: destabilize 强调使系统失去稳定。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The rebels tried to sabotage the negotiations.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 叛乱者试图破坏谈判。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: sabotage 强调故意破坏行动。

antonyms.example_sentence: The court upheld the constitutional principle.
antonyms.example_translation: 法院维护了这一宪法原则。
antonyms.difference_note: uphold 与 subvert 的破坏权威相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The reform preserved the core of the old system.
antonyms.example_translation: 改革保留了旧制度的核心。
antonyms.difference_note: preserve 强调让既有事物继续存在。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The village was submerged after the flood.
confusable_words.example_translation: 洪水过后村庄被淹没。
confusable_words.difference_note: submerge 是物理淹没，不是制度或观念上的颠覆。

extension.root_affix: sub- 表示“在下方”；vert 表示“转”。subvert 的画面是从下面转动、掏空一个体系的支撑。

word_family.note: 名词，常指颠覆既有制度的行为。
形容词或名词，指意图颠覆的人或思想。
同含 vert，表示转成相反方向。

related_words.note: con + vert，转向另一种状态或信仰。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 颠覆传统的; 颠覆既有事物的 seeking or intended to subvert an established system or institution: subversive literature 反传统文学 n. {iji#i4}- a subversive person: The government claims that the riots were incited by subversives. BUR F A BALA BDF aw fh. 派生 subversion /seb'va:fan/ n. 颠覆; 颠覆政权;颠覆传统 the undermining of the power and authority of an established system or institution: be arrested for subversion 因进行颠覆活动而被捕 Strive /strarv/ V.i， 努力; 奋斗 to devote serious effort or energy; endeavor: strive towards a goal 朝着一个目标奋斗 | strive for a breakthrough in cancer research 力争在癌症研究方面有突破

headline.core_en: intended to undermine an established system, authority, or convention

familiar_bridge.summary: subversive 来自 subvert：不是普通 rebellious，而是带着“动摇既有秩序”的力量。

core.main: subversive 可形容思想、作品、活动，也可指被认为会颠覆现有制度的人。

senses.label: 颠覆性的
senses.text: 意在削弱制度、权威或传统观念。

senses.label: 颠覆分子
senses.text: 指被视为从内部破坏体制的人。

usage_groups.group_title: 思想、作品或活动挑战既有秩序
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调某种内容不只是不同，而是会动摇权威、传统或规范。

examples.sentence: The play was considered subversive because it mocked the ruling class.
examples.translation: 这部戏被认为具有颠覆性，因为它嘲讽了统治阶层。
examples.teaching_note: subversive art/ideas 常出现在政治和文化评论中。

examples.sentence: Her subversive reading of the myth challenged the traditional moral lesson.
examples.translation: 她对这个神话的颠覆性解读挑战了传统道德寓意。
examples.teaching_note: subversive reading 指重新解释并动摇旧理解。

usage_groups.group_title: 被视为威胁体制的人
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 作名词时，指被当局或社会视为颠覆力量的人。

examples.sentence: The regime arrested writers it labeled as subversives.
examples.translation: 该政权逮捕了被它称为颠覆分子的作家。
examples.teaching_note: as subversives 表示这些人被贴上政治标签。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The students adopted a rebellious tone.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 学生们采取了反叛的语气。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: rebellious 可指反抗姿态，subversive 更强调动摇制度。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The reformers proposed a radical change to the voting system.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 改革者提出了对投票制度的根本性改变。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: radical 强调改变很彻底；subversive 更强调挑战或破坏既有秩序。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The government faced an insurgent movement in the north.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 政府在北方面临反叛运动。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: insurgent 更偏武装或政治反叛。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The pamphlet was denounced as seditious.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这本小册子被谴责为煽动叛乱。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: seditious 带有法律和政治指控色彩。

antonyms.example_sentence: The committee preferred conformist opinions.
antonyms.example_translation: 委员会更偏好顺从主流的意见。
antonyms.difference_note: conformist 与 subversive 的挑战秩序相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: His view remained orthodox despite new evidence.
antonyms.example_translation: 尽管有新证据，他的观点仍然正统。
antonyms.difference_note: orthodox 强调符合既有权威或传统。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The law was controversial but not subversive.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这项法律有争议，但并不具有颠覆性。
confusable_words.difference_note: controversial 只是引发争论，不一定动摇体制。

extension.root_affix: subversive 来自 subvert；-ive 使它成为表示倾向或性质的形容词。

related_words.note: 有时也挑战既有制度，但范围更宽。
侧重公开反对官方立场。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 不能容忍的; 让人难以忍受的 too extreme to bear; intolerable: insufferable insolence 无法容忍的傲慢 | an insufferable pore 让人受不了的讨厌鬼

headline.core_cn: 难以忍受的；令人受不了的
headline.core_en: too unpleasant, annoying, or extreme to bear

familiar_bridge.summary: suffer 是“忍受、遭受”；insufferable 就是让人几乎 suffer 不下去。

core.main: insufferable 常形容人、态度、噪音、热度或傲慢，让人感觉“实在受不了”。

senses.label: 难以忍受的
senses.text: 强烈到令人无法容忍。

senses.label: 令人讨厌得受不了的
senses.text: 常形容人的傲慢、自大或烦人表现。

usage_groups.group_title: 人或态度让人受不了
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调傲慢、烦人或自以为是到了令人难忍的程度。

examples.sentence: His insufferable arrogance made every meeting exhausting.
examples.translation: 他令人难以忍受的傲慢让每次会议都很累人。
examples.teaching_note: insufferable arrogance 是评价人品态度的自然搭配。

usage_groups.group_title: 环境或感受强烈到无法忍受
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 形容热、噪音、疼痛、等待等超过可忍耐范围。

examples.sentence: By noon the heat in the crowded room had become insufferable.
examples.translation: 到中午，拥挤房间里的热气已经让人难以忍受。
examples.teaching_note: insufferable heat 表示程度高到受不了。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The pain became unbearable at night.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 夜里疼痛变得无法忍受。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unbearable 最基础，适用范围广。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The noise was intolerable during the exam.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 考试期间噪音让人无法容忍。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: intolerable 更正式，也可用于制度或行为。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His obnoxious remarks offended several guests.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 他讨厌的言论冒犯了几位客人。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The law was attacked as an odious restriction on freedom.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这项法律被批评为对自由的可憎限制。

antonyms.example_sentence: The delay was inconvenient but tolerable.
antonyms.example_translation: 延误不方便，但还能忍受。
antonyms.difference_note: tolerable 与 insufferable 直接相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The cool evening was pleasant after the hot day.
antonyms.example_translation: 炎热一天之后，凉爽的夜晚令人舒服。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The historian documents the suffering caused by the famine.
confusable_words.example_translation: 报告描述了饥荒造成的痛苦。
confusable_words.difference_note: suffering 是名词，insufferable 是形容词，表示让人无法忍受。

extension.root_affix: in- 表示否定；suffer 表示忍受；-able 表示能够。insufferable 的构词逻辑是“不能被忍受的”。

word_family.note: 少见，但构词上与 insufferable 相对。

related_words.note: 与 suffer 的“忍受”接近。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 煽动性的 tending to stir up conflict: incendiary remarks 煽动性言论

headline.core_en: likely to inflame conflict, or designed to cause fire

familiar_bridge.summary: incendiary 的核心画面是“点火”：可以是真火，也可以是把情绪和冲突点燃。

core.main: GRE 和阅读中更常见的是比喻义：言论、文章、政策或行动煽动愤怒与冲突。

senses.label: 煽动性的
senses.text: 容易激起愤怒、冲突或暴力。

senses.label: 纵火的；燃烧性的
senses.text: 指用于引火或造成火灾的装置。

usage_groups.group_title: 言论或行动点燃冲突
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调言论或行动点燃冲突。

examples.sentence: The senator’s incendiary remarks turned a policy debate into a public outrage.
examples.translation: 这位参议员煽动性的言论把政策辩论变成了公众愤怒。
examples.teaching_note: incendiary remarks 强调言论像点火一样激化冲突。

usage_groups.group_title: 引火或纵火用途
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于具体设备或材料时，表示会造成火灾。

examples.sentence: Investigators found an incendiary device near the warehouse.
examples.translation: 调查人员在仓库附近发现了一个纵火装置。
examples.teaching_note: incendiary device 是实义“引火装置”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The headline was provocative but not entirely false.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个标题很挑衅，但并非完全不实。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: provocative 强调引发反应，incendiary 更像激化冲突。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The article used inflammatory language.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这篇文章使用了煽动性语言。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: inflammatory 与 incendiary 的比喻义很接近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The leaflet was condemned as seditious.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这份传单被谴责为煽动叛乱。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: seditious 更偏政治和法律语境。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The material is highly combustible.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这种材料高度易燃。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: combustible 只指物理燃烧，不指煽动言论。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her conciliatory tone helped calm the room.
antonyms.example_translation: 她调和的语气帮助平息了现场情绪。
antonyms.difference_note: conciliatory 与 incendiary 的激化冲突相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The mayor offered calming words after the protest.
antonyms.example_translation: 抗议后市长发表了安抚性讲话。
antonyms.difference_note: calming 强调降低紧张情绪。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The decision incensed the public.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这个决定激怒了公众。
confusable_words.difference_note: incense 作动词是激怒，incendiary 是形容词/名词，强调点燃冲突。

extension.root_affix: incendiary 和 incense, ignite 都属于“火、点燃”的语义场；记忆时抓住 fire 这个底层画面。

word_family.note: 作名词时可指人或装置。

related_words.note: 既可点火，也可激起情绪。
比喻义近似，常修饰 rhetoric/language。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. iiESE; 证明 confirm or give support to (a statement, theory, or finding): The witness corroborated the accused's statement. 证人证实了被告的陈述 。 | Do you have anyone who could corroborate that? 你能提供证人证明那件事吗 ? call for phrv. 需要; 要求 make necessary: Desperate times call for desperate measures. 紧急关头需要紧

headline.core_en: to support a claim, account, or theory with additional evidence

familiar_bridge.summary: corroborate 比 confirm 更偏“另有证据来支持”，常出现在证词、研究和调查中。

core.main: corroborate 的重点是补上一份独立证据，让原来的说法更可信。

senses.label: 证实；佐证
senses.text: 用证据、证词、数据或发现支持某个说法。

usage_groups.group_title: 用证据支持某个说法
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调额外证据让说法更可靠。

examples.sentence: The new documents corroborate the witness’s account of the meeting.
examples.translation: 这些新文件证实了证人对会议的描述。
examples.teaching_note: corroborate an account 表示用外部材料支持某个叙述。

examples.sentence: Later experiments corroborated the scientist’s original hypothesis.
examples.translation: 后来的实验佐证了这位科学家最初的假设。
examples.teaching_note: 科研语境中常用实验结果来 corroborate a hypothesis。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The test confirmed the diagnosis.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 测试确认了诊断。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: confirm 更常用，corroborate 更强调额外证据支持。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The data support the researcher’s conclusion.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 数据支持研究者的结论。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report substantiated the allegations.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 报告证实了这些指控。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: substantiate 强调给说法提供实质根据。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The results validated the model.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 结果验证了这个模型。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: validate 更偏证明方法、模型或结论有效。

antonyms.example_sentence: The evidence refuted the earlier claim.
antonyms.example_translation: 证据驳倒了早先的说法。
antonyms.difference_note: refute 与 corroborate 的支持方向相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The witness’s statement contradicted the video evidence.
antonyms.example_translation: 证人的陈述与视频证据相矛盾。
antonyms.difference_note: contradict 表示证据或说法互相冲突。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The two labs collaborated on the project.
confusable_words.example_translation: 两个实验室合作完成了这个项目。
confusable_words.difference_note: collaborate 是合作，corroborate 是证实；拼写相近但语义不同。

extension.root_affix: corroborate 源自“使更强”的意象；证据让一个说法更有力。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 简明的; 简洁的; 简练的 (especially of something written or spoken) briefly and clearly expressed: The newspaper published an announcement of his death in succinct terms. 报纸用简 En R aH TH NAIR. | write short, succinct sentences 写简练的短名近义词 concise

headline.core_en: briefly and clearly expressed

familiar_bridge.summary: succinct 不是单纯 short，而是短得清楚、有重点。

core.main: succinct 常评价解释、总结、发言或文字，强调没有废话，同时意思完整。

senses.label: 简明的；简洁的
senses.text: 用少量词语清楚表达重点。

usage_groups.group_title: 文字或发言简短清楚
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调表达压缩但不含糊。

examples.sentence: The editor asked for a more succinct summary of the argument.
examples.translation: 编辑要求把这个论证概括得更简明。
examples.teaching_note: succinct summary 是学术写作和编辑语境中的自然搭配。

examples.sentence: Her answer was succinct but complete enough to address the question.
examples.translation: 她的回答简洁，但足以回应这个问题。
examples.teaching_note: succinct 不是内容不足，而是表达不拖沓。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He gave a brief reply.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 他做了简短回答。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: brief 只强调短，succinct 还强调清楚。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The instructions were concise and easy to follow.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 说明简洁，容易照做。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: concise 与 succinct 很接近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her terse response ended the discussion.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 她简短生硬的回应结束了讨论。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: terse 可能带冷淡或不耐烦语气。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The critic offered a pithy description of the film.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 评论家对这部电影做了简短有力的描述。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: pithy 强调短而有力、有味道。

antonyms.example_sentence: The proposal was verbose and hard to follow.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个提案冗长，难以理解。
antonyms.difference_note: verbose 与 succinct 的简洁相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: His rambling speech lost the audience’s attention.
antonyms.example_translation: 他散漫冗长的发言让听众失去注意力。
antonyms.difference_note: rambling 强调结构松散、跑题。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The answer was short but unclear.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这个回答很短，但不清楚。
confusable_words.difference_note: short 不保证清楚；succinct 要短且清楚。

extension.root_affix: succinct 可和 concise, precise 一起记：都强调表达控制得好。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

(subject to) 受 … 影响的; 易于 … 的 likely or prone to be affected by (a particular condition or occurrence, typically an unwelcome or unpleasant one): My cousin is subject to panic attacks. 我的表弟容易惊慌失措 。 129

headline.core_en: a person or thing being discussed, studied, or represented

familiar_bridge.summary: subject 可以想成“被拿来谈、写、研究或观察的对象”。

core.main: 这里的 subject 是名词，重点是讨论、研究、描写的对象；不要和动词 subject /səbˈdʒekt/ 混在一起。

senses.label: 话题；主题
senses.text: 讨论、文章或作品围绕的内容。

senses.label: 研究对象；实验对象
senses.text: 被观察、测试或研究的人或事物。

senses.label: 主人公或描写对象
senses.text: 绘画、故事、照片中被表现的人或物。

usage_groups.group_title: 讨论或写作围绕的主题
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 表示文章、课程、争论或谈话的中心内容。

examples.sentence: The subject of the essay is the tension between freedom and security.
examples.translation: 这篇文章的主题是自由与安全之间的张力。
examples.teaching_note: subject of the essay 表示文章讨论的核心内容。

usage_groups.group_title: 被研究、测试或观察的对象
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 在科研、心理学、医学等语境中，subject 是被研究的人或对象。

examples.sentence: The study followed each subject for six months after the treatment.
examples.translation: 这项研究在治疗后跟踪每位受试者六个月。
examples.teaching_note: subject 在研究语境中常指受试者。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The topic of the lecture was migration.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 讲座的话题是移民。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: topic 更日常，subject 更宽，可指研究对象。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Justice is a central theme of the novel.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 正义是这部小说的核心主题。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: theme 常指文学或思想主题。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The object of the study was language development.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这项研究的对象是语言发展。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: object 更偏哲学、研究或目标语境。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Each participant completed a memory test.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 每位参与者完成了一项记忆测试。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: participant 只指参与研究的人。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The review was subjective rather than evidence-based.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这篇评论是主观的，而不是基于证据的。
confusable_words.difference_note: subjective 是形容词，表示主观；subject 作名词是主题或对象。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The workers were subjected to harsh conditions.
confusable_words.example_translation: 工人们被迫承受恶劣条件。
confusable_words.difference_note: subjected 来自动词 subject，读音和名词 subject 不同。

extension.root_affix: sub- 有“在下面”的感觉；ject 有“投、放”的感觉。subject 原本带有“被放在某种视线或讨论之下”的画面。

word_family.note: 从个人感受或观点出发。
常与 subject 构成主客体对照。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 题材;主题 the topic dealt with or the subject represented in a debate, exposition, or work of art: The movie has been rated “R” due to adult subject matter. 这部影片因其成人题材而被列为限制级

headline.core_en: the content or topic represented, discussed, or treated in a work

familiar_bridge.summary: subject matter 比 subject 更强调“作品、文章或课程实际处理的内容”。

core.main: subject matter 常用于艺术、文学、课程和学术讨论，指被表达或研究的材料与主题。

senses.label: 题材；主题内容
senses.text: 作品、文章、课程或研究所处理的内容。

usage_groups.group_title: 作品或文章处理的题材
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调一件作品讲的是什么，而不是它的形式或风格。
usage_groups.when_to_use: art, literature, essay, film, academic writing。
common_patterns: the subject matter of a painting

examples.sentence: Although the film uses comedy, its subject matter is deeply serious.
examples.translation: 虽然这部电影使用喜剧手法，但它的题材非常严肃。
examples.teaching_note: subject matter 指作品真正处理的内容。

examples.sentence: The subject matter of the painting is ordinary domestic life.
examples.translation: 这幅画的题材是普通家庭生活。
examples.teaching_note: 艺术评论中常用 subject matter 指画面内容。

usage_groups.group_title: 课程或研究覆盖的内容范围
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 说明某门课或某项研究实际包括哪些内容。

examples.sentence: The course covers complex subject matter, so students need some background in statistics.
examples.translation: 这门课内容复杂，因此学生需要一些统计学基础。
examples.teaching_note: complex subject matter 表示课程内容本身难。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The content of the course changes every year.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这门课的内容每年都会变化。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: content 更普通，subject matter 更正式。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The topic of the article is urban poverty.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这篇文章的话题是城市贫困。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: topic 通常更窄，subject matter 可指整个作品的题材范围。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Memory is a recurring theme in the novel.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 记忆是这部小说反复出现的主题。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The poet drew her material from village life.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这位诗人从乡村生活中汲取素材。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: material 可指创作或研究所用内容。

antonyms.example_sentence: The critic focused on form rather than subject matter.
antonyms.example_translation: 评论家关注的是形式，而不是题材内容。
antonyms.difference_note: form 是表达方式，subject matter 是内容。

antonyms.example_sentence: The painting’s style is modern, but its subject matter is traditional.
antonyms.example_translation: 这幅画风格现代，但题材传统。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The subject of the poem is exile.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这首诗的主题是流亡。
confusable_words.difference_note: subject 更基础；subject matter 更强调作品或课程的内容范围。

extension.root_affix: subject matter 是固定组合，成词根；学习时把它当作“内容/题材”这一整体表达。

word_family.note: 描述与主题相关的内容。

related_words.note: 常用于文学和艺术分析。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

需区分 subject 与 subject matter

需区分 subject 与 subject matter

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t， 娇生惯养; 溺爱 treat in an indulgent or overprotective way: She coddles her children. 她溺爱她的孩子

headline.core_en: to treat someone with too much care or protection

familiar_bridge.summary: coddle 比 care for 多了一层“保护过头”，像把人捧得太软、太经不起风雨。

core.main: coddle 常用于批评过度照顾孩子、学生或下属，让他们缺少独立面对困难的机会。

senses.label: 溺爱；过度保护
senses.text: 用过分纵容或保护的方式对待某人。

usage_groups.group_title: 过度保护孩子或学生
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调照顾过头，反而削弱对方独立能力。

examples.sentence: Parents who coddle their children may leave them unprepared for failure.
examples.translation: 过度保护孩子的父母可能让孩子无法面对失败。
examples.teaching_note: coddle children 带批评语气，不是普通照顾。

examples.sentence: The coach refused to coddle the team after a poor performance.
examples.translation: 球队表现糟糕后，教练拒绝继续惯着他们。
examples.teaching_note: refuse to coddle 表示不再纵容或过度保护。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The hotel pampers its guests with constant attention.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这家酒店用无微不至的服务宠着客人。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: pamper 可中性或正面，coddle 常带批评。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Too many gifts can spoil a child.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 太多礼物可能宠坏孩子。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: spoil 强调结果是把人宠坏。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher refused to mollycoddle students who ignored the assignment.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 老师拒绝娇惯那些不做作业的学生。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The parents indulged every demand their son made.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 父母满足了儿子的每一个要求。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: indulge 强调满足欲望，不一定有保护意味。

antonyms.example_sentence: The program disciplines students without humiliating them.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个项目在不羞辱学生的情况下训练他们。
antonyms.difference_note: discipline 与 coddle 的纵容相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The difficult journey hardened the young climbers.
antonyms.example_translation: 艰难旅程让年轻登山者变得坚强。
antonyms.difference_note: harden 强调让人更能承受困难。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Good teachers care for students without coddling them.
confusable_words.example_translation: 好老师关心学生，但不会过度保护他们。
confusable_words.difference_note: care 是必要关心，coddle 是过度保护。

extension.root_affix: coddle 的记忆重点不是词根，而是语气：它通常暗含“照顾得太多”。

word_family.meaning_cn: 被溺爱的；被过度保护的

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 断言; 坚称 state or assert to be the case: The man averred that he had nothing to do with the theft. 这人坚称他与这盗窃案无关 。 | He averred that he needed no help. 他自信地说他不需要任何帮助

headline.core_en: to state firmly that something is true

familiar_bridge.summary: aver 比 say 更正式，重点是明确、坚定地声称某事为真。

core.main: aver 常用于法律、正式陈述或书面语，表示某人坚称事实如此。

senses.label: 断言；坚称
senses.text: 以正式或坚定的方式说某事是真的。

usage_groups.group_title: 正式地坚称某事为真
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 常和 that 从句，用在证词、声明、论证或正式写作中。
usage_groups.when_to_use: legal statement, formal claim, written argument。
common_patterns: aver the truth of a claim

examples.sentence: The witness averred that she had seen the defendant leave the building.
examples.translation: 证人坚称自己看见被告离开了大楼。
examples.teaching_note: aver that... 是最自然的句法结构。

examples.sentence: The scholar averred that the manuscript was older than previously believed.
examples.translation: 这位学者断言该手稿比先前认为的更古老。
examples.teaching_note: 正式论证中可用 aver 表示坚定主张。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report states the main findings clearly.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 报告清楚陈述了主要发现。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: state 更普通，aver 更正式且更坚定。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The company claims the product is safe.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 公司声称该产品安全。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The author asserts that culture shapes perception.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 作者断言文化塑造感知。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: assert 与 aver 接近，但更常见。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The signature attests that the document is genuine.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 签名证明文件真实。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: attest 更强调作证或证明。

antonyms.example_sentence: He denied any involvement in the scheme.
antonyms.example_translation: 他否认参与了这个计划。
antonyms.difference_note: deny 与 aver 的肯定断言相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The official retracted the earlier statement.
antonyms.example_translation: 这位官员撤回了早先声明。
antonyms.difference_note: retract 是收回已经说过的话。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Quick action averted a larger crisis.
confusable_words.example_translation: 迅速行动避免了更大的危机。
confusable_words.difference_note: avert 多一个 t，是避免；aver 是断言。

extension.root_affix: aver 与 ver- 的“真实”有关，可和 verify, veracity 一起记。

word_family.note: 法律或正式语境中可见。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: aver: reviewed headline, usage_tree, relations, extension, and student-visible wording. Issues: OCR 音标和释义需清理；需补正式语境和容易混淆的词. Actions: 重写 usage group；补 aver/avert 区分；补 ver- 迁移.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 顺利的;有利的 conducive to success; favorable: made an auspicious beginning 取得开门红 #5044) favorable 派生 inauspicious /mo:xsplfas/ adj. 不顺利的; 不利的 not conducive to success; unpromising: The meeting was inauspicious. 那次会面很不顺利

headline.core_cn: 吉利的；有利的；预示成功的
headline.core_en: favorable and suggesting future success

familiar_bridge.summary: auspicious 不只是 lucky，而是一个开始、迹象或时机让人觉得后面会顺利。

core.main: auspicious 常形容 beginning、sign、occasion、moment，强调事情开端好、预示结果可能成功。

senses.label: 吉利的；有利的
senses.text: 带来好兆头或有助于成功。

usage_groups.group_title: 开端或迹象预示顺利
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 评价开始、时机、迹象是否对未来成功有利。

examples.sentence: The project had an auspicious beginning when its first trial succeeded.
examples.translation: 第一次试验成功，使这个项目有了一个吉利的开端。
examples.teaching_note: auspicious beginning 强调开头顺利，预示后续可能成功。

examples.sentence: The discovery came at an auspicious moment for the young field of research.
examples.translation: 这一发现出现在这个年轻研究领域的有利时刻。
examples.teaching_note: auspicious moment 表示时机对发展有利。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The negotiations began under favorable conditions.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 谈判在有利条件下开始。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: favorable 更普通，auspicious 更有“好兆头”意味。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The early results look promising.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 早期结果看起来很有希望。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The reform came at a propitious time.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 改革出现在一个有利时机。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: propitious 与 auspicious 很接近，更书面。

advanced_synonyms.meaning_cn: 有益的；有利健康或改进的
advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The criticism had a salutary effect on the institution.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 批评对这个机构产生了有益影响。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: salutary 强调结果有益，不一定有好兆头。

antonyms.example_sentence: The campaign got off to an inauspicious start.
antonyms.example_translation: 这场运动开局不顺。

antonyms.example_sentence: The team faced unfavorable conditions.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个团队面临不利条件。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The guard noticed a suspicious package.
confusable_words.example_translation: 警卫注意到一个可疑包裹。
confusable_words.difference_note: suspicious 与 auspicious 拼写相近，但意思完全不同。

extension.root_affix: auspicious 可和 inauspicious 成对记；前者是好兆头，后者是不利开端。

word_family.note: 常见于 under the auspices of。

related_words.note: 帮助理解“好兆头”的感觉。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 重新设计; 重新制订 design (something) again in a different way: redesign a machine 重新设计一台机器 | redesign a policy 重新制订政策

headline.core_en: to design something again in a new or different way

familiar_bridge.summary: re- 表示 again；redesign 就是在原有设计基础上重新做一版。

core.main: redesign 可用于机器、网站、建筑、制度、政策或流程，强调不是小修小补，而是重新安排结构或方案。

senses.label: 重新设计；重新制订
senses.text: 用新的方式重新设计某物或方案。

usage_groups.group_title: 重新设计产品、系统或政策
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调改变原有结构，使其更有效、更清晰或更适合新目的。

examples.sentence: The city redesigned the bus system to make transfers easier.
examples.translation: 这座城市重新设计了公交系统，使换乘更方便。
examples.teaching_note: redesign a system 表示重新安排整体结构。

examples.sentence: The company launched a complete redesign of its website.
examples.translation: 公司推出了网站的全面改版。
examples.teaching_note: redesign 也可作名词，指重新设计后的方案或过程。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The committee revised the proposal after public criticism.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 委员会在公众批评后修订了提案。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: revise 可以是局部修改，redesign 更偏整体设计重做。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She reworked the chapter to improve its structure.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 她重写了这一章以改善结构。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The agency overhauled its outdated procedures.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 该机构全面改造了过时流程。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: overhaul 比 redesign 更强调彻底整修。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The engineers reconfigured the network.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 工程师重新配置了网络。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: reconfigure 偏结构或设置的重新排列。

antonyms.example_sentence: The museum preserved the original layout.
antonyms.example_translation: 博物馆保留了原始布局。

antonyms.example_sentence: The new plan retains the old entrance.
antonyms.example_translation: 新方案保留了旧入口。
antonyms.difference_note: retain 强调继续保留某部分。

confusable_words.example_sentence: They decorated the room without changing its layout.
confusable_words.example_translation: 他们装饰了房间，但没有改变布局。
confusable_words.difference_note: decorate 只改变外观，redesign 改的是设计方案。

extension.root_affix: re- 表示 again；design 是设计。redesign 不只用于物品，也可用于制度、流程或系统。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: redesign: reviewed headline, usage_tree, relations, extension, and student-visible wording. Issues: 源例可用但范围偏窄；需区分 redesign/decorate/revise. Actions: 重写 usage group；补 re- 构词和相关 re- 词.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 剧烈的; 极端的 in a way that is likely to have a strong or far-reaching effect: drastic reductions in spending 消费锐减 | drastic measures 极端措施

headline.core_en: severe and likely to have a strong or far-reaching effect

familiar_bridge.summary: drastic 可以理解成 strong + far-reaching：变化或措施不仅大，而且影响范围深。

core.main: drastic 常形容 change、measure、reduction、action，强调幅度大、后果强，常带不得不采取强硬手段的感觉。

senses.label: 剧烈的；大幅度的
senses.text: 形容变化、下降、增长等幅度很大。

senses.label: 严厉的；激烈的
senses.text: 形容措施或行动强硬，影响大。

usage_groups.group_title: 变化幅度很大
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于数字、趋势、命运或状态发生明显变化。

examples.sentence: The new policy led to a drastic reduction in water use.
examples.translation: 新政策使用水量大幅减少。
examples.teaching_note: drastic reduction 强调下降幅度很大。

usage_groups.group_title: 措施强硬、影响深远
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于解决严重问题时采取的强烈手段。

examples.sentence: The school took drastic measures after the safety violations were discovered.
examples.translation: 安全违规被发现后，学校采取了严厉措施。
examples.teaching_note: drastic measures 表示措施强硬且影响明显。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The drought caused severe damage to crops.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 干旱对农作物造成严重损害。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: severe 强调严重程度，drastic 强调幅度或手段强。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The plan requires major changes.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个计划需要重大改变。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: major 更中性，drastic 更强烈。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The proposal called for radical reform.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 该提案要求根本性改革。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: radical 强调从根本上改变。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The law introduced sweeping changes.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这项法律带来了广泛变化。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report recommended moderate reforms.
antonyms.example_translation: 报告建议进行温和改革。
antonyms.difference_note: moderate 与 drastic 的强烈程度相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The update made only minor changes.
antonyms.example_translation: 这次更新只做了小改动。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The discovery produced a dramatic shift in opinion.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这一发现使观点发生显著转变。
confusable_words.difference_note: dramatic 强调显著或引人注目，drastic 强调强烈且影响深。

extension.root_affix: drastic 的学习重点在搭配：changes/reductions/measures/action。

word_family.note: 副词，常修饰 change, reduce, increase。

source.raw_ocr_text: adv. 剧烈的; 极端的 in a way that is likely to have a strong or far- reaching effect: Now her fortunes have changed drastically. 她的命运发生了剧变

headline.core_en: in a severe or far-reaching way

familiar_bridge.summary: drastically 是 drastic 的副词，常和 change、reduce、increase、improve 这类动词一起出现。

core.main: drastically 强调变化幅度大，常用于数据、处境、政策效果或生活状态突然明显改变。

senses.label: 剧烈地；大幅度地
senses.text: 以强烈、明显、影响深远的方式发生变化。

usage_groups.group_title: 变化大到能明显看出差别
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调变化大到能明显看出差别。
usage_groups.when_to_use: statistics, policy effects, personal fortune, conditions。

examples.sentence: Her fortunes changed drastically after the company went public.
examples.translation: 公司上市后，她的命运发生了剧烈变化。
examples.teaching_note: change drastically 强调前后差异很大。

examples.sentence: The new treatment drastically reduced the patients’ recovery time.
examples.translation: 这种新疗法大幅缩短了患者恢复时间。
examples.teaching_note: drastically reduce 常用于结果明显的变化。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The new evidence greatly strengthened the case.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 新证据大大加强了这个案件。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Prices rose sharply in winter.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 冬季价格急剧上涨。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: sharply 常用于数据快速变化。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The result changed dramatically after the final round.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 最后一轮后结果发生了显著变化。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: dramatically 强调显著或戏剧效果，drastically 强调幅度和影响。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The invention radically changed communication.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这项发明从根本上改变了交流方式。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: radically 更强调根本性质变化。

antonyms.example_sentence: The temperature rose slightly overnight.
antonyms.example_translation: 夜间气温略有上升。
antonyms.difference_note: slightly 与 drastically 的幅度相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The new design marginally improved performance.
antonyms.example_translation: 新设计使性能略有提升。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The actor paused dramatically before answering.
confusable_words.example_translation: 演员在回答前戏剧性地停顿了一下。
confusable_words.difference_note: dramatically 还可指戏剧化方式；drastically 不这样用。

extension.root_affix: drastically = drastic + -ly；它把形容词“剧烈的”变成修饰动作或变化的副词。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 【生物 ] 共生的 involving interaction between two different organisms living in close physical association: The fungi form symbiotic associations with the roots of plant species. 真菌和植物的根系形成了共生关系 。 adj. 互惠的; 互助的 denoting a mutually beneficial relationship between different people or groups: a symbiotic relationship 互惠关系

headline.core_en: involving a close and mutually beneficial relationship

familiar_bridge.summary: symbiotic 最早来自生物“共生”，后来也用来形容人、组织或制度之间相互依赖、彼此受益。

core.main: symbiotic 的关键是双方关系紧密，并且至少在典型用法中彼此有收益或依赖。

senses.label: 共生的
senses.text: 不同生物长期生活在一起，相互影响或互利。

senses.label: 互惠互助的
senses.text: 不同人、群体或机构之间形成彼此依赖的关系。

usage_groups.group_title: 生物之间形成共生关系
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于生态学，描述不同生物紧密生活在一起。

examples.sentence: The fungi form a symbiotic relationship with the roots of the plant.
examples.translation: 这些真菌与植物根部形成共生关系。
examples.teaching_note: symbiotic relationship 是生物语境中的核心表达。

usage_groups.group_title: 人或机构之间互相依赖、彼此受益
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于社会、经济、政治或文化评论，强调双方关系紧密。
common_patterns: a symbiotic relationship between A and B

examples.sentence: The media and political campaigns often have a symbiotic relationship.
examples.translation: 媒体和政治竞选活动常常形成相互依赖的关系。
examples.teaching_note: 这里的 symbiotic 是比喻用法，强调双方互相需要。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The agreement was based on mutual benefit.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这项协议建立在互利基础上。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: mutual 强调相互，symbiotic 强调关系紧密且相互依赖。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The two teams developed a cooperative approach.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 两个团队形成了合作方式。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: cooperative 强调合作，不一定长期依赖。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The two economies are deeply interdependent.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这两个经济体高度相互依赖。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: interdependent 与 symbiotic 的比喻义接近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The treaty created reciprocal obligations.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 该条约产生了相互义务。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: reciprocal 更强调双方对应交换。

antonyms.example_sentence: The relationship was parasitic rather than mutually beneficial.
antonyms.example_translation: 这种关系是寄生性的，而不是互惠的。
antonyms.difference_note: parasitic 是一方获益、一方受损。

antonyms.example_sentence: The two systems operate as independent units.
antonyms.example_translation: 这两个系统作为独立单位运行。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The gesture was symbolic rather than practical.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这个姿态是象征性的，而非实际性的。
confusable_words.difference_note: symbolic 是象征，symbiotic 是共生；拼写相近但不同。

extension.root_affix: sym- 有“共同、一起”的感觉；bio 与 life 有关。symbiotic 的原始画面是“共同生活”。

related_words.note: bio 也出现在与生命/人生相关的词中。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.i. 闲聊 totalk idly; chatter: They nattered away for an hour about nothing. 他们空谈了一个小时

headline.core_en: to talk casually or at length about unimportant things

familiar_bridge.summary: natter 接近 chatter，但更有“聊个不停、内容不太重要”的口语感。

core.main: natter 常用于轻松或略带不耐烦的语气，表示漫无目的地聊天、闲扯。

senses.label: 闲聊；絮叨
senses.text: 长时间谈论不重要的事情。

usage_groups.group_title: 漫无目的地闲聊
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调谈话轻松、零碎，常不涉及重要内容。

examples.sentence: They nattered away for an hour about old classmates.
examples.translation: 他们絮絮叨叨地聊了一个小时老同学的事。
examples.teaching_note: natter away 表示聊个不停。

examples.sentence: After the meeting, we had a quick natter over coffee.
examples.translation: 会后我们边喝咖啡边短暂闲聊了一会儿。
examples.teaching_note: have a natter 是英式口语。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: We chatted for a few minutes before class.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 上课前我们聊了几分钟。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: chat 最普通，natter 更口语且有闲扯感。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The children chattered excitedly on the bus.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 孩子们在公交车上兴奋地说个不停。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: chatter 可强调声音多或说个不停。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The host prattled on without answering the question.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 主持人喋喋不休，却没有回答问题。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: prattle 更贬义，内容显得幼稚或空洞。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He babbled nervously during the interview.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 面试中他紧张地语无伦次。

antonyms.example_sentence: The committee deliberated for three hours.
antonyms.example_translation: 委员会审议了三个小时。
antonyms.difference_note: deliberate 是严肃讨论，和 natter 的闲聊相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: He nagged his brother about cleaning the room.
confusable_words.example_translation: 他唠叨着责备弟弟打扫房间。
confusable_words.difference_note: nag 是责备式唠叨，natter 是闲聊。

extension.root_affix: natter 没有值得硬拆的词根；记忆重点是英式口语里的闲聊感。

related_words.note: 声音和说个不停的感觉更强。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: R74 ##L v.i. (meet with) 遇到; 遭遇; 得到 ( 情况、 态度 、 回应等 ) encounter or be faced with (a particular fate, situation, attitude, or reaction): We met with a slight setback. 我们遇到了一点 #44. | Her ideas met with their approval. 她的想法得到了他们的赞同 。 adj. 合适的; 恰当的 suitable; fit; proper: /t is a theater meet for great events. 这家剧院适合大场合 35444 condign

headline.core_en: to encounter a situation or reaction; suitable or proper

familiar_bridge.summary: 这里的 meet 不是最基础的“见面”，而是 meet with a reaction / meet with approval 这种正式结构，以及较少见的“合适的”。

core.main: GRE 语境要注意 meet with：想法、政策、行为“得到某种回应”或“遭遇某种情况”。形容词 meet = suitable/proper 较古雅。

senses.label: 遇到；遭遇；得到回应
senses.text: 常用于 meet with approval, resistance, difficulty。

senses.label: 合适的；恰当的
senses.text: 较正式或古雅的形容词用法，表示 suitable/proper。

usage_groups.group_title: 想法或行动得到某种回应
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调想法或行动得到某种回应。
usage_groups.when_to_use: reaction to ideas, plans, proposals, efforts。

examples.sentence: The proposal met with strong resistance from local residents.
examples.translation: 这个提案遭到当地居民的强烈反对。
examples.teaching_note: meet with resistance 表示遭遇反对。

examples.sentence: Her ideas met with approval from the committee.
examples.translation: 她的想法得到了委员会的认可。
examples.teaching_note: meet with approval 表示得到赞同。

usage_groups.group_title: 正式或古雅地表示合适
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 形容词 meet 表示适合某场合或目的，现在较少见。

examples.sentence: The hall was deemed meet for such a solemn ceremony.
examples.translation: 这座大厅被认为适合这样庄重的仪式。
examples.teaching_note: 形容词 meet 较古雅，阅读中识别即可。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The researchers encountered unexpected resistance.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 研究者遇到了意外阻力。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: encounter 可替代 meet with 的遭遇义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The plan received widespread approval.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个计划得到广泛认可。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: receive 更普通，meet with 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The speech elicited a strong response.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这场演讲引起强烈反应。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The quiet ceremony befitted the occasion.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这场安静的仪式与场合相称。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: befit 接近形容词 meet 的“合适”语义。

antonyms.example_sentence: The mayor avoided direct criticism of the policy.
antonyms.example_translation: 市长避免直接批评该政策。
antonyms.difference_note: avoid 与 meet with 的遭遇相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The joke was inappropriate for the ceremony.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个玩笑不适合这个仪式。
antonyms.difference_note: inappropriate 与形容词 meet 相反。

extension.root_affix: meet 的高阶用法主要靠短语结构，不靠词根；重点记 meet with + reaction。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. iff; 拍马屁 the fawning behavior of a sycophant; servile flattery: The fawning sycophancy is disgusting. FRAAH ES RILARN. 9% sycophantic /sikau'fentik/ adj. if ai); 拍马屁的 behaving or done in an obsequious way in order to gain advantage: sycophantic laughter i | sycophantic adulation R&R

headline.core_en: excessive flattery toward someone powerful in order to gain advantage

familiar_bridge.summary: sycophancy 比 flattery 更负面：不是普通夸赞，而是为了利益去讨好有权势的人。

core.main: sycophancy 常批评下属、追随者或政治人物对权力的卑躬屈膝。

senses.label: 谄媚；拍马屁
senses.text: 为了获得好处而过分奉承有权势者。

usage_groups.group_title: 为了利益讨好权力人物
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调奉承过度、姿态卑微，通常带强烈贬义。

examples.sentence: The minister’s public praise of the dictator was dismissed as pure sycophancy.
examples.translation: 这位部长公开赞美独裁者，被认为纯粹是在谄媚。
examples.teaching_note: sycophancy 强调对权力的讨好。

examples.sentence: The novel satirizes the sycophancy of officials who flatter anyone in power.
examples.translation: 这部小说讽刺了官员们对掌权者阿谀奉承的样子。
examples.teaching_note: satirize sycophancy 常见于政治或文学评论。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She recognized his flattery and ignored it.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 她看出他的奉承，没有理会。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: flattery 可轻可重，sycophancy 更卑屈、更功利。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The employee’s bootlicking annoyed his colleagues.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个员工的拍马屁让同事反感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His obsequiousness toward the director was embarrassing.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 他对主任的卑躬屈膝令人尴尬。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: obsequiousness 强调姿态过分顺从。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The essay attacks the toadyism common in the court.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这篇文章批评宫廷中常见的逢迎谄媚。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: toadyism 是较书面的贬义词。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her candor impressed the committee.
antonyms.example_translation: 她的坦率给委员会留下印象。
antonyms.difference_note: candor 与 sycophancy 的虚假奉承相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: His defiance angered the ruler.
antonyms.example_translation: 他的公然反抗激怒了统治者。

confusable_words.example_sentence: A sincere compliment can encourage a student.
confusable_words.example_translation: 真诚的赞美可以鼓励学生。
confusable_words.difference_note: compliment 可以真诚，sycophancy 是功利的谄媚。

extension.root_affix: sycophancy 的词族比词根更重要；记住 sycophant 是“拍马屁的人”。

related_words.note: 常形容讨好权力的姿态。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t， 为 … 悲痛; 哀悼; 痛惜 to feel or express great sadness or disappointment about sb./sth.: lament the sad result 4 E16 th 4 i #874 =| lament this great loss 痛惜这一巨大的损失

headline.core_en: to express grief, sorrow, or regret about a loss or bad outcome

familiar_bridge.summary: lament 比 regret 更有情感重量，常带公开表达悲伤或痛惜的感觉。

core.main: lament 可以是动词，也可以是名词，重点是对损失、失败、死亡或令人失望的结果表达悲痛和惋惜。

senses.label: 哀悼；痛惜
senses.text: 为某种损失或不幸结果感到并表达悲伤。

senses.label: 悲叹；哀歌
senses.text: 作名词时指悲伤的表达或文字。

usage_groups.group_title: 为损失或失败表达悲痛
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调对失去、衰落或不幸结果表达悲伤、惋惜或批评。

examples.sentence: The writer lamented the loss of local traditions in modern cities.
examples.translation: 这位作家痛惜现代城市中地方传统的消失。
examples.teaching_note: lament the loss of... 表示对失去之物的惋惜。

examples.sentence: Her poem is a quiet lament for a childhood home that no longer exists.
examples.translation: 她的诗是一首安静的哀歌，悼念已经不存在的童年家园。
examples.teaching_note: lament 作名词时常指带悲伤情绪的作品或表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The nation mourned the victims of the disaster.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 全国哀悼灾难中的遇难者。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: mourn 常用于死亡或重大损失。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She regretted missing the chance to speak.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 她遗憾错过了发言机会。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: regret 更日常，lament 更有公开悲叹感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The essay bewails the disappearance of public trust.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这篇文章哀叹公共信任的消失。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The committee deplored the use of violence.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 委员会强烈谴责使用暴力。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: deplore 可带道德谴责，lament 更偏悲伤惋惜。

antonyms.example_sentence: The festival celebrates the region’s cultural diversity.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个节日庆祝该地区的文化多样性。
antonyms.difference_note: celebrate 与 lament 的悲伤方向相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The crowd rejoiced at the news.
antonyms.example_translation: 人群听到消息后欢欣鼓舞。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Residents complained about the noise.
confusable_words.example_translation: 居民抱怨噪音。
confusable_words.difference_note: complain 是表达不满，lament 更强调悲伤或痛惜。

extension.root_affix: lament 词根；记忆重点是“悲伤地表达”。

word_family.note: 名词，常指强烈或正式的哀叹。
形容词，评价事情糟糕到令人惋惜。
常形容逝者或失去之物。

source.raw_ocr_text: vt. Zerit}; 尖叫 to utter or emit an acute piercing sound: shrill out a high E flat 尖声唱出一声高音降 B

headline.core_en: high-pitched and piercing; to make such a sound

familiar_bridge.summary: shrill 的声音不是单纯 high，而是高到刺耳、让人不舒服。

core.main: shrill 常形容声音尖锐刺耳，也可比喻言论或批评尖刻、激烈。

senses.label: 尖厉刺耳的
senses.text: 声音高而尖，让人觉得刺耳。

senses.label: 尖声叫出
senses.text: 作动词时表示发出尖锐声音。

senses.label: 尖刻激烈的
senses.text: 比喻言论或批评过于刺耳。

usage_groups.group_title: 声音尖锐刺耳
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调声音尖锐刺耳。

examples.sentence: A shrill whistle cut through the noise of the station.
examples.translation: 一声尖锐的哨音穿过车站的嘈杂声。
examples.teaching_note: shrill whistle 强调声音高而刺耳。

examples.sentence: The child shrilled in fear when the door slammed shut.
examples.translation: 门砰地关上时，孩子害怕地尖叫起来。
examples.teaching_note: shrill 作动词时表示尖声叫出。

usage_groups.group_title: 言论或批评显得尖刻刺耳
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调言论或批评显得尖刻刺耳。

examples.sentence: The debate became shrill as both sides stopped listening to each other.
examples.translation: 双方不再倾听彼此后，辩论变得尖刻刺耳。
examples.teaching_note: 比喻义中 shrill 批评语气过激。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: A piercing scream came from the hallway.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 走廊里传来一声刺耳尖叫。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The machine made a high-pitched sound.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 机器发出高频声音。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: high-pitched 更中性，shrill 更刺耳。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The article adopted a strident tone.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这篇文章采用了尖锐强硬的语气。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: strident 常用于声音或言论，语气强硬。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Raucous laughter filled the bar.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 酒吧里充满了刺耳喧闹的笑声。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: raucous 更粗哑喧闹，不一定高音。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her mellow voice calmed the audience.
antonyms.example_translation: 她柔和的声音安抚了观众。
antonyms.difference_note: mellow 与 shrill 的尖锐刺耳相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: A soft melody played in the background.
antonyms.example_translation: 背景中响起轻柔的旋律。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The sharp sound made everyone turn around.
confusable_words.example_translation: 尖锐的声音让所有人转过头。
confusable_words.difference_note: sharp 可形容声音、边缘或思维；shrill 更专注刺耳高音。

extension.root_affix: shrill 主要靠声音画面记忆，不必硬拆词根。

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 使适应; 使符合 make receptive or aware: The company is attuned to the needs of its customers. 公司符合消费者的需求

headline.core_en: to make someone receptive to or aware of something

familiar_bridge.summary: tune 是调音；attune 像把人的感觉、判断或系统调到某个频率上。

core.main: attune 常见于 be attuned to，表示对需求、变化、情绪或细微信号很敏感、能适应。

senses.label: 使适应；使协调
senses.text: 让某人与环境、需求或节奏相适应。

senses.label: 使敏锐感知
senses.text: 让人能注意到细微变化或信号。

usage_groups.group_title: 对需求或变化保持敏感
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 常用被动结构 be attuned to，表示能察觉并回应。

examples.sentence: Good teachers are attuned to the needs of different students.
examples.translation: 好老师能敏锐感知不同学生的需要。

examples.sentence: The critic was attuned to subtle shifts in the author’s tone.
examples.translation: 这位评论家能敏锐察觉作者语气的细微变化。
examples.teaching_note: attuned to subtle shifts 常用于阅读和评论语境。

usage_groups.group_title: 让人或系统适应新的环境
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调调整，使之与某个环境、节奏或目标一致。
common_patterns: attune a system to local conditions

examples.sentence: The training attuned new employees to the culture of the organization.
examples.translation: 培训让新员工适应了这个组织的文化。
examples.teaching_note: attune someone to... 表示把人调整到某种环境中。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The team adapted quickly to the new rules.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 团队很快适应了新规则。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: adapt 更普通，attune 更强调感知和协调。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She adjusted her schedule to fit the new deadline.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 她调整了日程以适应新截止日期。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The course sensitized students to cultural differences.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这门课让学生对文化差异更敏感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The program helped refugees acclimate to the city.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个项目帮助难民适应城市生活。

antonyms.example_sentence: The policy alienated many loyal supporters.
antonyms.example_translation: 这项政策疏远了许多忠实支持者。
antonyms.difference_note: alienate 与 attune 的协调适应相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Repeated exposure can desensitize viewers to violence.
antonyms.example_translation: 反复接触会让观众对暴力麻木。
antonyms.difference_note: desensitize 与 attune 的敏锐感知相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The musician tuned the guitar before the concert.
confusable_words.example_translation: 音乐家在演出前给吉他调音。
confusable_words.difference_note: tune 是具体调音或调整，attune 更抽象。

extension.root_affix: attune 来自 tune 的“调音”画面；抽象地说，就是把感知或反应调到合适状态。

word_family.note: 最常见形态，多用 be attuned to。

related_words.note: 帮助理解 tune 的协调感。
语义上接近 attune to subtle signals。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 热切的; 热情的 having or showing eagerness or enthusiasm: keen to help 乐意帮忙 i£ X74 eager adj. 敏锐的 sharp or penetrating: keen eyesight 敏锐的眼光

headline.core_en: eager and enthusiastic; sharp or highly perceptive

familiar_bridge.summary: keen 有两条常见线：人很 eager，感官或头脑很 sharp。

core.main: keen 既可表示热切愿意做某事，也可表示眼光、感官、头脑敏锐。

senses.label: 热切的；渴望的
senses.text: 对某事有兴趣、有热情。

senses.label: 敏锐的；锋利的
senses.text: 形容眼光、感觉、理解力或竞争很强。

usage_groups.group_title: 对某事很感兴趣、很愿意
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 常用 be keen to do 或 be keen on。

examples.sentence: Several students were keen to join the research project.
examples.translation: 几名学生很想加入这个研究项目。
examples.teaching_note: be keen to do 表示热切愿意做某事。

usage_groups.group_title: 眼光、感官或判断很敏锐
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调眼光、感官或判断很敏锐。

examples.sentence: Her keen insight helped the team identify the flaw in the argument.
examples.translation: 她敏锐的洞察力帮助团队发现了论证中的漏洞。
examples.teaching_note: keen insight 是评价思维敏锐的自然搭配。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The volunteers were eager to begin.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 志愿者们急切地想开始。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: eager 对应 keen 的热切义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She has a sharp eye for detail.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 她对细节有敏锐眼光。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: sharp 对应 keen 的敏锐义。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The scientist had an acute sense of the problem’s importance.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这位科学家敏锐意识到问题的重要性。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: acute 更正式，常指感知或问题严重。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her perceptive comments changed the discussion.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 她有洞察力的评论改变了讨论。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: perceptive 偏理解人或事的能力。

antonyms.example_sentence: He seemed indifferent to the outcome.
antonyms.example_translation: 他似乎对结果漠不关心。
antonyms.difference_note: indifferent 与 keen 的热切相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: A dull mind may miss the hidden irony.
antonyms.example_translation: 迟钝的头脑可能看不出隐藏的讽刺。
antonyms.difference_note: dull 与 keen 的敏锐相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: She was kind to the new student.
confusable_words.example_translation: 她对新学生很友善。
confusable_words.difference_note: kind 和 keen 发音相近但意思不同。

extension.root_affix: keen 没有必要硬拆词根；学习时把 eager 和 sharp 两条义线分清。

source.raw_ocr_text: #474) FL adj. 后天习得的 caused by environmental forces (as use and disuse) and not subject to transmission from parent to offspring: acquired reaction 后天反应

headline.core_en: gained or developed after birth, experience, or exposure rather than inherited

familiar_bridge.summary: acquire 是“获得”；acquired 强调某种能力、特征或状态不是天生，而是后来形成的。

core.main: acquired 常和 skill、taste、trait、condition 搭配，和 innate/inherited 形成对比。

senses.label: 后天获得的；习得的
senses.text: 通过经验、环境、学习或接触获得，而不是遗传而来。

usage_groups.group_title: 能力、品味或特征后天形成
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调经验、训练或环境造成。

examples.sentence: Appreciating bitter tea can be an acquired taste.
examples.translation: 欣赏苦茶可能是一种后天养成的品味。
examples.teaching_note: acquired taste 指一开始不一定喜欢，后来慢慢学会欣赏。

examples.sentence: The study distinguishes inherited traits from acquired traits.
examples.translation: 这项研究区分遗传特征和后天获得的特征。
examples.teaching_note: acquired trait 与 inherited trait 对照。

usage_groups.group_title: 疾病或状态在出生后获得
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 医学或生物语境中，强调不是先天遗传。

examples.sentence: The patient developed acquired immunity after repeated exposure to the virus.
examples.translation: 患者在反复接触病毒后形成了获得性免疫。
examples.teaching_note: acquired immunity 表示后天获得的免疫力。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The behavior may be learned rather than instinctive.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这种行为可能是学来的，而不是本能的。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She developed a habit of reading before bed.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 她养成了睡前阅读的习惯。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The animal showed a conditioned response to the sound.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 动物对这个声音表现出条件反应。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: conditioned 常用于心理学或行为学。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The disorder appears to be nonhereditary.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这种疾病似乎不是遗传性的。

antonyms.example_sentence: Some fears may be innate.
antonyms.example_translation: 有些恐惧可能是天生的。
antonyms.difference_note: innate 与 acquired 直接对立。

antonyms.example_sentence: The child inherited her mother’s eye color.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个孩子遗传了母亲的眼睛颜色。

confusable_words.example_sentence: A passport is required for the trip.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这次旅行需要护照。
confusable_words.difference_note: required 是必须的，acquired 是获得的。

extension.root_affix: acquire 表示获得；acquired 是它的过去分词形容词。

word_family.note: 名词，语言习得也用 acquisition。

related_words.note: quire/quer 有寻找、寻求的感觉。
词形远亲，注意不要和 acquired 混。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj.&adv. 人均的; 人均地 for each person; in relation to people taken individually: the richest nation per capita 人均最富有的国家 | a per capita gross national product 人均国民生产总值

headline.core_en: for each person in a population

familiar_bridge.summary: per 是“每”，capita 和 head 有关；per capita 就是“按每个人平均”。

core.main: per capita 常用于经济、人口、资源、收入和产出数据，帮助比较不同规模的国家或群体。

senses.label: 人均的；人均地
senses.text: 按每个人计算或平均分配。

usage_groups.group_title: 按人口平均比较经济或资源数据
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于收入、产出、消费、排放等统计。

examples.sentence: The country has a high GDP per capita despite its small population.
examples.translation: 尽管人口少，这个国家的人均 GDP 很高。
examples.teaching_note: GDP per capita 用于按人口平均比较经济水平。

examples.sentence: Water use per capita fell after the city introduced conservation rules.
examples.translation: 城市出台节水规定后，人均用水量下降了。
examples.teaching_note: per capita 可放在名词后，表示按每人计算。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The cost is fifty dollars per person.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 费用是每人五十美元。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: per person 更口语，per capita 更正式。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The average income rose last year.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 平均收入去年上升。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: average 不一定按人口计算。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Population-adjusted figures give a fairer comparison.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 按人口调整后的数据能提供更公平的比较。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 用于解释统计比较，较技术化。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The per-head cost of the program was low.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个项目的人均成本很低。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: per-head 较口语或技术，含义接近 per capita。

antonyms.example_sentence: Aggregate income can hide large differences among individuals.
antonyms.example_translation: 总收入可能掩盖个体之间的巨大差异。

antonyms.example_sentence: Total emissions rose even as emissions per capita fell.
antonyms.example_translation: 尽管人均排放下降，总排放仍然上升。
antonyms.difference_note: total 与 per capita 的统计角度不同。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The company needed more capital to expand.
confusable_words.example_translation: 公司需要更多资本来扩张。
confusable_words.difference_note: capita 不是 capital；per capita 是统计短语。

extension.root_affix: capita 来自 caput/head 的“人头”意象；per capita 字面就是“按每个人头”。

word_family.note: 主要在 per capita 中出现。
de + caput，帮助理解 cap/head 的古老词根。

related_words.note: per year, per person 都是类似结构。
per capita 常用于人口统计比较。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.i. (FOES SEPA) 死亡; 灭亡 die, especially in a violent or sudden way: perish from disease 病逝 | perish in battle KB 3H 派生 perishable /perrfabl/ adj. 转瞬即逝的;容易消亡的 (of something abstract) having a brief life or significance; transitory: Ballet is the most perishable of arts. 芭某舞是最胞弱的艺术

headline.core_en: to die or be destroyed, especially suddenly or violently

familiar_bridge.summary: perish 比 die 更书面，常用于灾难、战争、寒冷、饥荒或抽象事物的消亡。

core.main: perish 既可指人或生命死亡，也可指艺术、记忆、希望等脆弱事物消逝。

senses.label: 死亡；丧生
senses.text: 尤指因灾难、战争、疾病或恶劣条件突然死亡。

senses.label: 消亡；毁灭
senses.text: 抽象事物短暂存在后消失。

usage_groups.group_title: 在灾难、战争或恶劣条件中死亡
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 比 die 更正式、更有悲剧感。

examples.sentence: Many soldiers perished in the final months of the war.
examples.translation: 许多士兵在战争最后几个月中丧生。
examples.teaching_note: perish in war/battle 是正式叙述中的自然表达。

usage_groups.group_title: 脆弱事物短暂存在后消亡
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于艺术、记忆、希望或影响等抽象内容。

examples.sentence: Some traditions perish when no one passes them on to the next generation.
examples.translation: 如果没有人传给下一代，一些传统就会消亡。
examples.teaching_note: perish 可用于抽象事物的消失。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Many plants died during the drought.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 许多植物在干旱中死去。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: die 最普通，perish 更正式、更有灾难感。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The custom gradually disappeared.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个习俗逐渐消失了。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: disappear 用于消失，不一定有毁灭或死亡意味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The old law expired at midnight.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 旧法律在午夜失效。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: expire 可指生命结束或期限到期。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Several patients succumbed to the infection.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 几名患者死于感染。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: succumb 常接 to，强调没能抵抗住。

antonyms.example_sentence: The village survived the flood.
antonyms.example_translation: 村庄在洪水中幸存下来。
antonyms.difference_note: survive 与 perish 直接相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The tradition endured for centuries.
antonyms.example_translation: 这一传统延续了几个世纪。
antonyms.difference_note: endure 强调经受住时间或困难。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Fresh fruit is perishable and must be stored carefully.
confusable_words.example_translation: 新鲜水果易腐，必须妥善储存。
confusable_words.difference_note: perishable 是形容词，表示容易腐坏或短暂。

extension.root_affix: perish 的派生词 perishable 很重要：容易坏、容易消逝。

word_family.note: 形容词，可指食物或抽象艺术。
im- 否定，表示不会轻易消逝。

related_words.note: 帮助理解抽象事物易消逝。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 一时的风尚; 一时的狂热 an intense and widely shared enthusiasm for something, especially one that is short-lived and without basis in the object's qualities; a craze: No one plays that game anymore; it was only a fad. 没有人再玩那种游戏了 ， 它只流行了一阵子 | adopt the latestjad 趋附最新的时尚

headline.core_en: a fashion or enthusiasm that becomes popular quickly and fades quickly

familiar_bridge.summary: fad 像 fashion 的短命版本：突然流行，热度很快过去。

core.main: fad 常用于批评某种流行现象缺少持久价值，只是短暂跟风。

senses.label: 一时风尚；短暂狂热
senses.text: 短时间内很多人追捧，但很快消退的事物。

usage_groups.group_title: 突然流行又很快退潮
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调流行速度快、持续时间短，常暗示价值不深。
usage_groups.when_to_use: fashion, diet, game, technology trend, youth culture。

examples.sentence: The app was everywhere for a month, but it turned out to be a passing fad.
examples.translation: 这个应用火了一个月，但后来证明只是一阵短暂风潮。
examples.teaching_note: passing fad 强调热度很快过去。

examples.sentence: Critics dismissed the diet as a fad rather than a serious health practice.
examples.translation: 批评者认为这种饮食法只是一时潮流，而不是严肃的健康方法。
examples.teaching_note: diet fad 常带怀疑或批评语气。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Remote work became a major trend after the pandemic.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 疫情后远程办公成为重要趋势。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: trend 可持续较久，fad 更短命。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The fashion spread quickly among teenagers.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这种时尚迅速在青少年中传播。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: fashion 范围广，fad 更强调短暂狂热。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The toy craze swept through the country.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这股玩具热席卷全国。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: craze 更强调很多人疯狂追捧。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Minimalist design was in vogue for several years.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 极简设计流行了好几年。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: vogue 更书面，可能持续更久。

antonyms.example_sentence: The novel became a classic rather than a passing fad.
antonyms.example_translation: 这部小说成了经典，而不是一时风尚。

antonyms.example_sentence: Rice remains a mainstay of the local diet.
antonyms.example_translation: 米饭仍是当地饮食的主食。
antonyms.difference_note: mainstay 强调长期稳定存在。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Some fashions become fads, while others last for decades.
confusable_words.example_translation: 有些时尚会变成短暂风潮，有些则持续几十年。
confusable_words.difference_note: fad 是更短、更容易退潮的 fashion。

extension.root_affix: fad 词根；重点抓住 sudden popularity + short life。

related_words.note: 比 fad 更强调集体狂热。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 断言; 坚持声称 state as a fact; assert strongly and publicly: The two men affirm that they are innocent. AA PRA LEAEN. v.t. HESE; 确认; 支持 validate, confirm: The general affirmed rumors of an attack. ##iESX J # 击的传闻

headline.core_en: to state firmly, confirm, or support something as true

familiar_bridge.summary: affirm 可以理解成 make firm：把一个说法、信念或判断“立稳”。

core.main: affirm 既可表示公开坚定地说某事是真的，也可表示确认或支持某个原则、判决、身份或信念。

senses.label: 断言；坚持声称
senses.text: 坚定公开地说某事为真。

senses.label: 确认；支持
senses.text: 认可某个说法、原则、判决或价值。

usage_groups.group_title: 坚定声称某事为真
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 常和 that 从句，语气比 say 更正式。

examples.sentence: The two witnesses affirmed that the meeting had taken place.
examples.translation: 两名证人确认会议确实举行过。
examples.teaching_note: affirm that... 表示正式确认或断言。

usage_groups.group_title: 确认或支持原则、判决、价值
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 多出现在法律、伦理、政治或身份表达。

examples.sentence: The court affirmed the lower court’s decision.
examples.translation: 法院维持了下级法院的判决。
examples.teaching_note: 法律中 affirm a decision 表示确认或维持判决。

examples.sentence: The speech affirmed the importance of free inquiry.
examples.translation: 这场演讲肯定了自由探究的重要性。
examples.teaching_note: affirm the importance/value of... 表示公开支持某种价值。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The email confirmed the appointment.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 邮件确认了预约。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: confirm 更普通，affirm 更带正式支持或断言。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The author asserts that memory is unreliable.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 作者断言记忆并不可靠。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The results validated the new method.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 结果验证了新方法。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The court upheld the law.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 法院维持了这项法律。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: uphold 与 affirm 在法律语境接近。

antonyms.example_sentence: The official denied the accusation.
antonyms.example_translation: 官员否认了指控。
antonyms.difference_note: deny 与 affirm 的肯定相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The leader repudiated the earlier agreement.
antonyms.example_translation: 领导人否认早先协议的效力。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The test confirmed the prediction.
confusable_words.example_translation: 测试证实了预测。
confusable_words.difference_note: confirm 更偏事实验证，affirm 可偏价值立场。

extension.root_affix: firm 有“稳固”的感觉；affirm 是把说法、信念或判决稳稳立住。

related_words.note: 帮助记住 affirm 的“使坚定”感。
con + firm，也含使稳固的感觉。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. (BIA) 肯定的; 同意的; 认可的 agreeing with a statement or to a request: an affirmative answer 肯定的答复近义词 positive

headline.core_en: expressing agreement, approval, or confirmation

familiar_bridge.summary: affirmative 来自 affirm，意思是“给出肯定、确认或同意”。

core.main: affirmative 常修饰 answer、response、vote、action，表示明确同意或肯定。

senses.label: 肯定的；同意的
senses.text: 表示同意、认可或确认。

senses.label: 肯定答复
senses.text: 作名词时可指 yes 或肯定回答。

usage_groups.group_title: 明确给出同意或肯定答复
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于回答、投票、回应、证据等语境。

examples.sentence: When asked whether the data supported the claim, she gave an affirmative answer.
examples.translation: 当被问及数据是否支持这一主张时，她给出了肯定答复。
examples.teaching_note: affirmative answer 是最直接的搭配。

examples.sentence: The committee voted in the affirmative after a brief discussion.
examples.translation: 短暂讨论后，委员会投了赞成票。
examples.teaching_note: in the affirmative 是正式表达，表示“以肯定方式”。

usage_groups.group_title: 主动支持或确认某种价值
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于 affirmative action 等固定政策表达时，表示积极确认或扶持。

examples.sentence: The debate over affirmative action centered on fairness and historical disadvantage.
examples.translation: 关于平权行动的辩论集中在公平和历史性劣势上。
examples.teaching_note: affirmative action 是固定政策表达，不是普通“肯定行动”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She gave a positive response to the invitation.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 她对邀请给出了肯定回应。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His answer was a clear yes.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 他的回答是明确的同意。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: yes 是口语直接回答，affirmative 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The director gave an approving nod.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 主任赞许地点了点头。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The second test provided confirmatory evidence.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 第二次测试提供了确认性证据。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: confirmatory 多用于证据或测试。

antonyms.example_sentence: The answer was negative.
antonyms.example_translation: 回答是否定的。
antonyms.difference_note: negative 是 affirmative 的直接反义。

antonyms.example_sentence: The judge wrote a dissenting opinion.
antonyms.example_translation: 法官写了一份异议意见。
antonyms.difference_note: dissenting 强调不同意多数意见。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The statement affirmed the group’s commitment to reform.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这份声明确认了该组织改革的承诺。
confusable_words.difference_note: affirm 是动词，affirmative 是形容词或名词。

extension.root_affix: affirmative = affirm + -ative，表示带有肯定或确认性质。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. Hf to affirm again especially so as to strengthen or confirm: She reaffirmed her stance on the issue. 她重申了在这一问题的立场

headline.core_en: to affirm again, especially to strengthen or renew a position

familiar_bridge.summary: re- 表示 again；reaffirm 就是把已经说过或相信的立场再次确认。

core.main: reaffirm 常用于立场、承诺、信念、原则或判决，强调在质疑、变化或压力下再次表明。

senses.label: 重申；再次确认
senses.text: 再次确认某个立场、承诺、信念或决定。

usage_groups.group_title: 再次确认立场或承诺
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调某个态度或原则并没有改变。

examples.sentence: The mayor reaffirmed her commitment to protecting public parks.
examples.translation: 市长重申了保护公共公园的承诺。
examples.teaching_note: reaffirm a commitment 表示再次公开确认承诺。

examples.sentence: The court reaffirmed the principle that speech should not be restricted without clear cause.
examples.translation: 法院再次确认了言论不应在缺少明确理由时受到限制这一原则。
examples.teaching_note: reaffirm a principle 常见于法律和公共论述。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He repeated his promise to return.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 他再次说会回来。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: repeat 更普通，reaffirm 更强调重新确认立场。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The email confirmed the date of the interview.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 邮件确认了面试日期。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: confirm 不一定是再次确认。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The scholar reasserted the importance of evidence.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这位学者重申了证据的重要性。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: reassert 更强调重新提出主张。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The court upheld the earlier ruling.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 法院维持了早先判决。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: uphold 在法律语境中可接近 reaffirm a decision。

antonyms.example_sentence: The official retracted his earlier statement.
antonyms.example_translation: 官员撤回了早先声明。
antonyms.difference_note: retract 与 reaffirm 的再次确认相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: She renounced her former beliefs.
antonyms.example_translation: 她放弃了从前的信念。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The statement affirmed the group’s support for reform.
confusable_words.example_translation: 声明确认了该组织对改革的支持。
confusable_words.difference_note: affirm 是确认；reaffirm 是再次确认。

extension.root_affix: re- 表示 again；affirm 与 firm 有关，表示把某个立场立稳。

related_words.note: 同样有 firm 的稳固感。
语义接近，偏重新提出主张。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 病态的; 过度的; 过分的 being such to a degree that is extreme, excessive, or markedly abnormal: She has a pathological fear of heights. #75 SHAH.

headline.core_en: related to disease, or so extreme that it is abnormal

familiar_bridge.summary: pathology 是“病理学”；pathological 可以是真正医学上的病态，也可以比喻某种行为过度到不正常。

core.main: pathological 常形容 fear、lying、behavior、condition，强调异常、过度、像病一样难以控制。

senses.label: 病理的；病态的
senses.text: 与疾病或病理状态有关。

senses.label: 极端异常的；过度的
senses.text: 行为或情绪过强，到了不正常的程度。

usage_groups.group_title: 医学或心理上的病理状态
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调医学或心理上的病理状态。

examples.sentence: The patient’s pathological fear of contamination made daily life difficult.
examples.translation: 患者对污染的病态恐惧让日常生活变得困难。
examples.teaching_note: pathological fear 表示恐惧强到异常。

usage_groups.group_title: 行为或习惯过度到不正常
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调行为或习惯过度到不正常。

examples.sentence: The report described him as a pathological liar who invented stories even when there was no benefit.
examples.translation: 报告称他是一个病态说谎者，即使没有好处也会编故事。
examples.teaching_note: pathological liar 是固定而自然的表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The test showed abnormal results.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 测试显示结果异常。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: abnormal 更普通，pathological 更像病理或过度异常。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His reaction seemed extreme.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 他的反应显得极端。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: extreme 只表示程度很高，不一定病态。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She had a morbid fascination with disaster stories.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 她对灾难故事有一种病态的迷恋。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: morbid 常形容心理兴趣不健康。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His compulsive checking made work slow.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 他强迫性地反复检查，使工作变慢。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: compulsive 强调难以控制的冲动。

antonyms.example_sentence: The doctor said the reaction was normal.
antonyms.example_translation: 医生说这种反应正常。
antonyms.difference_note: normal 与 pathological 的异常相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: A healthy fear of risk can be useful.
antonyms.example_translation: 对风险有健康的畏惧可能是有用的。
antonyms.difference_note: healthy 可与病态心理形成对照。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The argument is logical but incomplete.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这个论证合乎逻辑但不完整。
confusable_words.difference_note: pathological 不是 path + logical 的简单组合，和 logical 无关。

extension.root_affix: patho- 与 disease/suffering 有关；-logy 是学科。pathological 来自 pathology 的病理语义。

related_words.note: pathos 有 suffering/feeling 的远源联系，帮助理解 patho 的情感和病痛语义。

需补 pathological liar/fear 用法

需补 pathological liar/fear 用法
